title: Euphorbia major
taxonomy: Euphorbia major is classified in the family Euphorbiaceae, which is a large and diverse family of flowering plants. This family is commonly known as the spurge family. Euphorbia major belongs to the genus Euphorbia, which includes over 2,000 species of plants. The genus Euphorbia is native to tropical and subtropical regions around the world. These plants are known for their unique and often succulent growth forms.

parts: Euphorbia major is commonly known as the pencil tree or pencil cactus. Euphorbia major has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The latex of the plant is considered the medicinal part and is used to treat various ailments such as fever, sore throat, and skin conditions. The latex is rich in flavonoids and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In some African cultures, the latex is used as a treatment for snake bites and other poisonous wounds.

intro: Euphorbia major, also known as Greater Caper Spurge, is a perennial plant native to the Mediterranean region, characterized by its milky sap and clusters of small, greenish-yellow flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in the treatment of wounds and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of Euphorbia major include diterpenoid esters, flavonoids, and phytosterols, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, the plant is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, and its extracts are also used in topical preparations to treat skin conditions and promote wound healing.
